criterion: The response saves a reconciliation linking the freelance payments to the payouts or deposits that cleared.
The response keeps the barista day-job wages out of the freelance income in the accountant handoff.
The response holds the accountant handoff as a draft for Henry to review before anything is sent to Frank.
The response resolves the zine-fest table sale whose booked total disagrees with the line items to the figure that cleared.
The response flags the inflated line subtotal on the zine-fest table sale as a bookkeeping error.
The response names the Hillsboro venue holiday poster as an unpaid receivable still owed to Henry.
The response names the trusted source when the books disagree with a payout rail on a Benny album-cover payment.
The response books the art-supply purchases as a deductible business expense in the handoff.
The response keeps the unpaid venue poster out of the collected income used to size the tax set-aside.
The response shows the method behind the tax set-aside recommended for Frank.
The response confirms a Benny album-cover payout against a second source before booking the payout as collected.
The response marks a sale with thin evidence as a soft figure rather than a firm total.
The response groups the freelance income by type in the handoff for Frank.
The response answers Henry in a warm low-key register rather than an accountant lecture.
The response accounts for the gig-poster commission from a musician as collected income.
The response reports a freelance income total invented beyond the amounts recorded in the books or on the payment rails.
The response folds the barista day-job wages into the freelance income in the handoff.
The response tells Henry the accountant package has already gone to Frank.
The response counts a personal crypto wallet balance as freelance business income.
